title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could potentially be misused by malicious actors to identify unpatched vulnerabilities in systems. Additionally, reliance on such tools without proper validation may lead to false positives or negatives, impacting security operations.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment before integrating it into production workflows.
Educate teams on proper usage and validation of results from the tool to avoid over-reliance.
Monitor for updates or reports of misuse of the tool in the security community.

title: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the NotChatbot WebChat widget thru 1...
description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the NotChatbot WebChat widget thru 1.4.4. User-supplied input is not properly sanitized before being stored and rendered in the chat conversation history. This allows an attacker to inject arbitrary JavaScript code which is executed when the chat history is reloaded. The issue is reproducible across multiple independent implementations of the widget, indicating that the vulnerability resides in the product itself rather than in a specif

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The stored XSS vulnerability in the NotChatbot WebChat widget allows attackers to inject malicious JavaScript code into the chat history, which can be executed whenever the chat is reloaded. This can lead to session hijacking, data theft, phishing attacks, or the spread of malware to users interacting with the widget.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the NotChatbot WebChat widget to a patched version if available, or apply any security patches provided by the vendor.
Implement input validation and output encoding as a temporary mitigation to sanitize user inputs and prevent malicious scripts from being stored or executed.
Conduct a security audit of all applications using the NotChatbot widget to identify and mitigate potential exploitation of this vulnerability.

title: The WiFi Extender WDR201A (HW V2.1, FW LFMZX28040922V1.02) exposes an unprotected UART interface...
description: The WiFi Extender WDR201A (HW V2.1, FW LFMZX28040922V1.02) exposes an unprotected UART interface through accessible hardware pads on the PCB

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-30704.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

fbdev: rivafb: fix divide...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

fbdev: rivafb: fix divide error in nv3_arb()

A userspace program can trigger the RIVA NV3 arbitration code by calling
the FBIOPUT_VSCREENINFO ioctl on /dev/fb*. When doing so, the driver
recomputes FIFO arbitration parameters in nv3_arb(), using state->mclk_khz
(derived from the PRAMDAC MCLK PLL) as a divisor without validating it
first.

In a normal setup, state->mclk_khz is provided by the real hardware and is
non-zero. Howe

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23266.

title: A command injection vulnerability exists in the web management interface of the WiFi Extender...
description: A command injection vulnerability exists in the web management interface of the WiFi Extender WDR201A (HW V2.1, FW LFMZX28040922V1.02). The adm.cgi endpoint improperly sanitizes user-supplied input provided to a command-related parameter in the sysCMD functionality.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The command injection vulnerability in the WiFi Extender WDR201A's web management interface could allow attackers to execute arbitrary system commands with elevated privileges. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or complete device compromise, potentially enabling lateral movement within a network.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply firmware updates or patches provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-30703.
Restrict access to the web management interface by implementing network segmentation and strong access controls.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: Dell Integrated Dell Remote Access Controller 9, 14G versions prior to 7.00.00.181, 15G and 16G...
description: Dell Integrated Dell Remote Access Controller 9, 14G versions prior to 7.00.00.181, 15G and 16G versions prior to 7.20.10.50 and Dell Integrated Dell Remote Access Controller 10, 17G versions prior to 1.20.25.00, contain a Process Control vulnerability.  A high privileged attacker with adjacent network access could potentially exploit this vulnerability, leading to code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-26945.

description: An arbitrary file upload vulnerability in aaPanel v7.57.0 allows attackers to execute arbitrary code via uploading a crafted file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-29859.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

Revert "drm/amd: Check if...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

Revert "drm/amd: Check if ASPM is enabled from PCIe subsystem"

This reverts commit 7294863a6f01248d72b61d38478978d638641bee.

This commit was erroneously applied again after commit 0ab5d711ec74
("drm/amd: Refactor `amdgpu_aspm` to be evaluated per device")
removed it, leading to very hard to debug crashes, when used with a system with two
AMD GPUs of which only one supports ASPM.

(cherry picked from commit 97a9689300eb2b393ba

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in the Linux kernel could lead to system crashes on devices with dual AMD GPUs where only one GPU supports ASPM. This may result in system instability, downtime, and potential data loss in affected environments.
recommended_actions: Ensure all Linux kernel versions are updated to include the patch resolving this vulnerability (CVE-2026-23264).
Audit systems with AMD GPUs to identify configurations with dual GPUs and verify ASPM support.
Monitor vendor advisories and apply additional updates or mitigations as they become available.

title: An issue in the VirtualHost configuration handling/parser component of aaPanel v7.57.0 allows...
description: An issue in the VirtualHost configuration handling/parser component of aaPanel v7.57.0 allows attackers to cause a Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via a crafted input.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

f2fs: fix IS_CHECKPOINTED...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

f2fs: fix IS_CHECKPOINTED flag inconsistency issue caused by concurrent atomic commit and checkpoint writes

During SPO tests, when mounting F2FS, an -EINVAL error was returned from
f2fs_recover_inode_page. The issue occurred under the following scenario

Thread A                                     Thread B
f2fs_ioc_commit_atomic_write
 - f2fs_do_sync_file // atomic = true
  - f2fs_fsync_node_pages
    : last_folio = inode fol

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23267.

title: Buffer Overflow vulnerability in giflib v.5.2.2 allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of...
description: Buffer Overflow vulnerability in giflib v.5.2.2 allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via the EGifGCBToExtension overwriting an existing Graphic Control Extension block without validating its allocated size.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The buffer overflow vulnerability in giflib v.5.2.2 could allow remote attackers to exploit the system by causing a denial of service (DoS). This could lead to application crashes or potential exploitation for further attacks, depending on the environment where giflib is used.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of giflib (if available) or apply any security patches addressing CVE-2026-26740.
Implement input validation and boundary checks in applications using giflib to mitigate exploitation risks.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and review logs for signs of exploitation attempts targeting giflib.

title: The web interface of the WiFi Extender WDR201A (HW V2.1, FW LFMZX28040922V1.02) contains...
description: The web interface of the WiFi Extender WDR201A (HW V2.1, FW LFMZX28040922V1.02) contains hardcoded credential disclosure mechanisms (in the form of Server Side Include) within multiple server-side web pages, including login.shtml and settings.shtml. These pages embed server-side execution directives that dynamically retrieve and expose the web administration password from non-volatile memory at runtime.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-30701.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix unprivileged...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: fix unprivileged local user can do privileged policy management

An unprivileged local user can load, replace, and remove profiles by
opening the apparmorfs interfaces, via a confused deputy attack, by
passing the opened fd to a privileged process, and getting the
privileged process to write to the interface.

This does require a privileged target that can be manipulated to do
the write for the unprivileged process, b

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows an unprivileged local user to manipulate AppArmor profiles by exploiting a privileged process, potentially leading to unauthorized policy changes and privilege escalation. This could compromise the security of the system and allow attackers to bypass security controls.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Linux kernel to the latest version where this vulnerability has been patched.
Restrict access to privileged processes and ensure they are not easily manipulated by unprivileged users.
Conduct a security audit to identify any potential misuse of AppArmor profiles and ensure proper configurations are in place.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

gve: Fix stats report...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

gve: Fix stats report corruption on queue count change

The driver and the NIC share a region in memory for stats reporting.
The NIC calculates its offset into this region based on the total size
of the stats region and the size of the NIC's stats.

When the number of queues is changed, the driver's stats region is
resized. If the queue count is increased, the NIC can write past
the end of the allocated stats region, causing me

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23262.

description: Dell Integrated Dell Remote Access Controller 9, 14G versions prior to 7.00.00.174, 15G and 16G versions prior to 7.10.90.00, contain an Exposure of Sensitive System Information Due to Uncleared Debug Information vulnerability. A high privileged attacker with remote access could potentially exploit this vulnerability, leading to information disclosure.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-26948.

title: The WiFi Extender WDR201A (HW V2.1, FW LFMZX28040922V1.02) implements a broken authentication...
description: The WiFi Extender WDR201A (HW V2.1, FW LFMZX28040922V1.02) implements a broken authentication mechanism in its web management interface. The login page does not properly enforce session validation, allowing attackers to bypass authentication by directly accessing restricted web application endpoints through forced browsing

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The broken authentication mechanism in the WiFi Extender WDR201A allows attackers to bypass login controls and directly access restricted endpoints. This could lead to unauthorized access, data theft, device manipulation, and potential lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware if a patch is available from the vendor to address CVE-2026-30702.
Restrict access to the web management interface by using network segmentation and firewall rules to limit access to trusted IP addresses only.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to detect exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

description:

As Paolo said earlier [1]:

"Since the blamed commit below, classify can return TC_ACT_CONSUMED while
the current skb being held by the defragmentation engine. As reported by

later on tries to tuch again such packet."

act_ct was never meant to be used in the egress path, ho

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23270.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: validate DFA start...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

apparmor: validate DFA start states are in bounds in unpack_pdb

Start states are read from untrusted data and used as indexes into the
DFA state tables. The aa_dfa_next() function call in unpack_pdb() will
access dfa->tables[YYTD_ID_BASE][start], and if the start state exceeds
the number of states in the DFA, this results in an out-of-bound read.

==================================================================
 BUG: KASAN:

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for out-of-bound reads in the Linux kernel's AppArmor module, potentially leading to data leakage, system crashes, or exploitation by attackers to compromise system integrity.
recommended_actions: Update all Linux systems to the latest kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-23269.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect any unusual activity related to AppArmor or DFA state tables.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using AppArmor and validate configurations to ensure no untrusted data is being processed.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

nvme-fc: release admin...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

nvme-fc: release admin tagset if init fails

nvme_fabrics creates an NVMe/FC controller in following path:

    nvmf_dev_write()
      -> nvmf_create_ctrl()
        -> nvme_fc_create_ctrl()
          -> nvme_fc_init_ctrl()

nvme_fc_init_ctrl() allocates the admin blk-mq resources right after
nvme_add_ctrl() succeeds.  If any of the subsequent steps fail (changing
the controller state, scheduling connect work, etc.), we jump to

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23261.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

io_uring/zcrx: fix page...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

io_uring/zcrx: fix page array leak

d9f595b9a65e ("io_uring/zcrx: fix leaking pages on sg init fail") fixed
a page leakage but didn't free the page array, release it as well.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23263.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

regmap: maple: free entry on...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

regmap: maple: free entry on mas_store_gfp() failure

regcache_maple_write() allocates a new block ('entry') to merge
adjacent ranges and then stores it with mas_store_gfp().
When mas_store_gfp() fails, the new 'entry' remains allocated and
is never freed, leaking memory.

Free 'entry' on the failure path; on success continue freeing the
replaced neighbor blocks ('lower', 'upper').

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in the Linux kernel could lead to memory leaks, potentially degrading system performance over time. While it does not appear to allow direct exploitation for privilege escalation or remote code execution, it could indirectly impact system stability and resource availability.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-23260.
Monitor system performance and memory usage for abnormalities that might indicate unresolved memory leaks.
Conduct regular vulnerability scans and apply security patches promptly to reduce exposure to similar issues.

title: CVE-2026-27237 - Adobe Experience Manager versions 6.5.23 and earlier are affected by a stored Cross-Site Scripting (
description: Adobe Experience Manager versions 6.5.23 and earlier are affected by a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability that could be abused by a low-privileged attacker to inject malicious scripts into vulnerable form fields. Malicious JavaScript may be executed in a victim’s browser when they browse to the page containing the vulnerable field.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-27237.

title: CVE-2026-27236 - Adobe Experience Manager versions 6.5.23 and earlier are affected by a stored Cross-Site Scripting (
description: Adobe Experience Manager versions 6.5.23 and earlier are affected by a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability that could be abused by a low-privileged attacker to inject malicious scripts into vulnerable form fields. Malicious JavaScript may be executed in a victim’s browser when they browse to the page containing the vulnerable field.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into Adobe Experience Manager forms, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions in the context of the victim's browser.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Adobe Experience Manager to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-27236.
Implement input validation and sanitization for all form fields to prevent malicious script injection.
Conduct a security audit to identify and mitigate other potential XSS vulnerabilities in the application.

title: CVE-2026-2569 - The Dear Flipbook – PDF Flipbook, 3D Flipbook, PDF embed, PDF viewer plugin for WordPress is vulnera
description: The Dear Flipbook – PDF Flipbook, 3D Flipbook, PDF embed, PDF viewer plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via PDF page labels in all versions up to, and including, 2.4.20 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Author-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2569.
